summary: Plan 15A is implementation-ready after the shared signed-tier/Wardline baseline. The repaired plan specifies a strict byte-oriented text sink with a closed stateless codec set, injected diversion policy, race-safe collision claims, atomic multi-batch replacement, append rollback, exact hashing, catalog parity, and round-trip proof.

review_basis.head: 07fc10b42fa0f8683630f249489368b5b072f8ff
review_basis.process: Independent reality, quality/security, and final executable-plan review against live sink contracts and the approved universal-policy design.

warnings.detail: Write-mode os.replace is the commit point; a later parent-directory fsync failure reports durability uncertainty and cannot restore the pre-run target.
A zero-row write with no target returns the existing sink-contract virtual zero-byte descriptor without creating a file; tests and documentation pin that consequence.

resolved_findings.resolution: All diversion tests inject on_write_failure, with a separate missing-injection fail-closed regression.
The abstract intermediate is never committed; the first green commit has concrete lifecycle methods and a verified source hash.
The sink uses a closed ASCII-compatible codec set and stages exact bytes, avoiding TextIOWrapper cookies and stateful encoders.
Write mode uses same-directory temporary files, copy-forwards prior owned batches, and preserves the old target through every pre-commit failure.
fail_if_exists and auto_increment reserve targets with O_EXCL and have concurrency regressions.
Append rollback is byte-based, closes/reopens after failure, and validates canonical LF with no CR anywhere.
Empty and all-diverted semantics, SinkCapabilities spelling, staging allowlists, and AST source-hash verification are explicit.

verification: live BaseSink diversion and SinkWriteResult contracts inspected
live CSV/Text source, SinkCapabilities, discovery, and plugin-hash helper contracts reconciled
Markdown fences balanced and git diff --check clean

review_history.summary: Initial review found unrunnable diversion tests, abstract/hash-invalid checkpoints, unsafe overwrite/append rollback, stateful encoding offsets, weak target validation, race-prone collision handling, and incomplete staging.
